issues.description: An in-flight token refresh can repopulate `cachedToken` after `disconnectSocket()` has cleared the cache. `disconnectSocket()` sets `pendingToken = null`, but the already-running promise still assigns `cachedToken` when `fetchSocketToken()` resolves. A later mount/session can then reuse that stale token without refetching, including after logout/login as a different user.
issues.recommendation: Add a token-cache generation/epoch or abort guard. Increment it in `disconnectSocket()`, capture it when starting `pending`, and only write `cachedToken` if the generation still matches. Add a regression test where `getSocketAuthToken()` is pending, `disconnectSocket()` runs, the first fetch resolves, and the next call must fetch a new token instead of returning the stale one.
